extraction.one_line_summary: SKJELMOSE won Tour de Suisse with Stage 8 ITT defense, exponentially improving after second time check to hold off AYUSO's rocket final descent
tactical_patterns: SKJELMOSE exponentially improved between second check and finish, reversing early deficit by pacing conservatively then surging late
AYUSO took 8 seconds from EVENEPOEL on final descent, revealing descending TT speed as tactical weapon for young GC riders
ASGREEN's 30-second gap to compatriot KRAGH ANDERSEN at same stage shows form divergence within national TT specialist pool

rider_signals.SKJELMOSE Mattias: positive: virtual yellow lost at first check but exponential improvement to finish shows exceptional TT pacing discipline under pressure
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: positive: fastest final sector taking 8s from EVENEPOEL on descent confirms technical descending as GC asset beyond climbing
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: won stage but lost 9s to SKJELMOSE reveals rare vulnerability when defending versus attacking TT mode
rider_signals.GALL Felix: negative: expected GC threat faded in TT, caught by SKJELMOSE starting 2min behind, confirms pure climber limitation
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: points jersey holder couldn't challenge EVENEPOEL's time despite specialist status, signals suboptimal condition
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: positive: fastest at all intermediate checks before EVENEPOEL, shows early aggression pays in undulating TT format
